Address: Zhongshan Dong Yi Road, Huangpu District, Shanghai
Category: Historical and cultural block, urban landscape

Ticket Purchase: No ticket required, some sightseeing projects require separate purchase
Introduction: The Bund is located on the Huangpu River bank in Huangpu District, central Shanghai, and is one of the most symbolic attractions of Shanghai's urban identity. Stretching 1.5 kilometers, it starts from Yan'an East Road in the south and ends at the Waibaidu Bridge on Suzhou Creek in the north, with the Huangpu River to the east and the former financial and foreign trade institutions of old Shanghai to the west. The Bund features 52 buildings of diverse classical revival styles, renowned as the 'Exhibition of International Architecture on the Bund,' representing important modern historical sites and representative architecture in China, and is one of Shanghai's key landmarks.
Suitable For: Families, couples, history and culture enthusiasts, photography lovers
Selling Points: Iconic riverfront skyline, Historic colonial architecture, Stunning night views, Cultural heritage landmark, Vibrant river cruise experience

Address: No. 132 Anren Street, Huangpu District, Shanghai
Ticket Price: Peak season (April 1 to June 30, September 1 to November 30 annually): adults 40 yuan, children 20 yuan; off-season (December 1 to March 31 of the following year, July 1 to August 31): adults 30 yuan, children 15 yuan
Ticket Purchase: Tickets available via Yuyuan official mini-program, on-site ticket windows, or third-party travel platforms
Introduction: Yuyuan Garden is located in the northeastern part of Shanghai's Old City, north of Fuyou Road, east of Anren Street, and southwest adjacent to the City God Temple and Yuyuan Mall. It is a Jiangnan classical garden originally built during the Jiajing and Wanli periods of the Ming Dynasty, covering over thirty mu. The garden features the famous Yulinglong rock, known as one of the Three Famous Stones of Jiangnan, and the Dianchun Hall, which served as the command center of the 1853 Small Sword Society uprising. Nearby are the City God Temple and shopping streets. Yuyuan Garden opened to the public in 1961 and was listed as a national key cultural relic protection unit by the State Council in 1982.
Suitable For: Family visitors, culture enthusiasts, photography lovers, historical researchers
Selling Points: Ancient classical garden with unique rock formations; Rich cultural heritage and historical sites; Traditional Chinese architecture and landscaping; Perfect place to experience local Shanghai culture; Beautiful lantern displays during festivals

Introduction: Lujiazui is Shanghai's financial center, home to numerous skyscrapers including the Shanghai Tower, Jin Mao Tower, and Shanghai World Financial Center, forming a stunning skyline. It is not only a commercial hub but also a famous tourist destination where visitors can take Huangpu River sightseeing cruises, visit the Shanghai Ocean Aquarium, and stroll along the Bund riverside promenade to enjoy views of the Bund.
Suitable For: Family outings, business professionals, photography enthusiasts, urban sightseeing tourists
Selling Points: Iconic skyline with supertall skyscrapers; Breathtaking night light shows; World-class financial hub; River cruises with Bund views; Family-friendly attractions like aquariums

Introduction: The Oriental Pearl Radio & TV Tower is one of Shanghai's iconic cultural landmarks, standing approximately 468 meters tall, adjacent to the Huangpu River and facing the Bund across the water. Construction began on July 30, 1991, and it officially opened to the public on November 18, 1994. The tower features a space cabin, rotating restaurant, and Shanghai Urban History Development Exhibition Hall, among other attractions and facilities, forming a multifunctional tourism complex integrating urban sightseeing, fashionable dining, shopping and entertainment, historical exhibitions, Huangpu River cruises, and conference performances.
Suitable For: Family outings, couples' dates, photography lovers, history and culture enthusiasts
Selling Points: Iconic skyline views; Revolving fine dining; Interactive history museum; Dazzling night shows; Riverfront convenience

Nearby Transport: Metro Line 10, Shanghai Library Station; Metro Line 11, Jiaotong University Station
Introduction: Wukang Road is located in Xuhui District, Shanghai. Originally named Ferguson Road, it was built in 1907 and is one of Shanghai's most distinctive streets. It preserves numerous historical buildings, including Spanish-style and French Renaissance-style old villas such as the Wukang Mansion, Ba Jin's former residence, and Huang Xing's former residence, earning it the reputation as a 'celebrity road' that 'condenses Shanghai's modern history of nearly a century.' The road is lined with plane trees, full of the rich charm of old Shanghai, making it an ideal place for strolling, photography, and experiencing Shanghai's history and culture.
Suitable For: History and culture enthusiasts, photography lovers, leisure walkers
Selling Points: Historic old buildings, Celebrity former residences, French Concession charm, Tree-lined scenic road, Authentic Shanghai vibe

Introduction: Tianzifang is a creative industry cluster transformed from Shanghai's unique Shikumen architecture, preserving the old Shanghai lane atmosphere while integrating modern art elements, featuring numerous galleries, studios, cafes, and specialty shops, and is one of the most artistically vibrant landmarks in Shanghai.
Suitable For: Artistic youth, photography lovers, history and culture enthusiasts
Selling Points: Shikumen architecture with modern art vibe,Boutique galleries and craft shops,Authentic Shanghai lane culture,Cozy cafes and local snack stalls,Ideal spot for photography and art exploration

Introduction: Founded in 1952, the Shanghai Museum is a large museum of ancient Chinese art, housing nearly one million cultural relics, of which 120,000 are fine pieces, particularly renowned for bronzes, ceramics, calligraphy, and paintings. The museum's architecture symbolizes 'heaven is round, earth is square,' and it is one of Shanghai's cultural landmarks.
Suitable For: History and culture enthusiasts, families, students
Selling Points: World-renowned bronze collection, Millennium-spanning ceramics, Precious calligraphy and paintings, Unique "round sky, square earth" architecture, Free admission for all

Address: No. 1686 Nanjing West Road, Jing'an District, Shanghai
Ticket Purchase: On-site purchase or online platform reservation
Introduction: Jing'an Temple is a famous ancient temple in Shanghai, originally built in the 10th year of Chiwu during the Eastern Wu period of the Three Kingdoms (247 AD), making it one of the oldest Buddhist temples in Shanghai. The temple's architecture blends features of Tibetan and Han Chinese Buddhism, with main structures including the Mahavira Hall, Hall of Heavenly Kings, and Hall of the Three Saints. It also houses precious Buddhist relics, such as a Ming Dynasty statue of Shakyamuni Buddha and a Qing Dynasty jade Buddha.
Suitable For: Religious and cultural enthusiasts, history lovers, tourists
Selling Points: A millennium-old Buddhist temple, A peaceful oasis in downtown Shanghai, Unique fusion of Tibetan and Han Buddhist architecture, Home to precious ancient Buddhist relics, Centrally located with easy access

Introduction: Zhujiacao Ancient Town is located in Qingpu District, Shanghai, and is one of Shanghai’s four major historical and cultural towns, with over 1,700 years of history. The town features a network of rivers, well-preserved Ming and Qing dynasty architecture, and numerous historical sites including the City God Temple, Fishing Release Bridge, and Kezhi Garden, earning it the nickname 'Venice of Shanghai'.
Suitable For: Family outings, history and culture enthusiasts, photography lovers
Selling Points: 1700-year-old water town charm, Well-preserved Ming & Qing architecture, Scenic river cruises, Authentic local snacks, Historic Chenghuang Temple

Introduction: Tianmen Mountain is the highest peak in Zhangjiajie, named after the natural wonder Tianmen Cave. It features the world’s longest high-altitude passenger cableway, glass skywalks, and other iconic landscapes, and is acclaimed as the 'First Divine Mountain of Western Hunan'.
Suitable For: Family outings, hiking enthusiasts, photography lovers
Selling Points: World's longest cable car ride; Thrilling glass skywalk; Iconic Tianmen Cave; Breathtaking mountain views; Rich Taoist culture
